title: Getting Started with nysOpenData
text: Welcome nysOpenData package, R package dedicated helping R users connect NY State Data Portal! nysOpenData package provides streamlined interface accessing New York State’s vast open data resources. connects directly NY State Open Data Portal, helping users bridge gap raw APIs tidy data analysis. two ways:

title: Getting Started with nysOpenData
text: primary way pull data package nys_pull_dataset() function, works tandem nys_list_datasets(). need know anything API keys authentication. first step call nys_list_datasets() see datasets list available use nys_pull_dataset() function. provides information thousands datasets found portal. output includes columns dataset title, description, link source. important pieces key uid. need either order use nys_pull_dataset() function. can put either key value uid value dataset = filter inside nys_pull_dataset(). instance, want pull dataset Lottery Cash 4 Life Winning Numbers: Beginning 2014, can use either methods : matter put uid key value dataset =, successfully get data!
code: nys_list_datasets() |> head() #> # A tibble: 6 × 18 #>   key        uid   title accessLevel landingPage issued `@type` modified keyword #>   <chr>      <chr> <chr> <chr>       <chr>       <chr>  <chr>   <chr>    <list>  #> 1 state_of_… 22ew… Stat… public      https://da… 2016-… dcat:D… 2026-04… <chr>   #> 2 open_ny_q… 22ie… Open… public      https://da… 2022-… dcat:D… 2025-06… <chr>   #> 3 lobbying_… 238s… Lobb… public      https://da… 2018-… dcat:D… 2023-07… <chr>   #> 4 mta_subwa… 23fs… MTA … public      https://da… 2026-… dcat:D… 2026-06… <chr>   #> 5 alternati… 23ry… Alte… public      https://da… 2023-… dcat:D… 2026-03… <NULL>  #> 6 nys_thruw… 23v4… NYS … public      https://da… 2017-… dcat:D… 2026-04… <chr>   #> # ℹ 9 more variables: identifier <chr>, description <chr>, distribution <list>, #> #   theme <list>, `contactPoint.@type` <chr>, contactPoint.fn <chr>, #> #   contactPoint.hasEmail <chr>, `publisher.@type` <chr>, publisher.name <chr> nys_cash_for_life_uid <- nys_pull_dataset(   dataset = "kwxv-fwze", limit = 2)  nys_cash_for_life_key <- nys_pull_dataset(   dataset = "lottery_cash_4_life_winning_numbers_2014_2026_retired_game", limit = 2)

title: Getting Started with nysOpenData
text: easiest workflow use nys_list_datasets() together nys_pull_dataset(). However, ample datasets portal, new ones added time, list datasets. event particular dataset want use R list, can use nys_any_dataset(). requirement dataset’s API endpoint (URL provided nys Open Data portal). steps get : NY State Open Data Portal, go dataset want work . Click “Export” (next actions button right hand side). Click “API Endpoint”. Click “SODA2” “Version”. Copy API Endpoint. example use nys_any_dataset() API endpoint discovered, pull data nys_pull_dataset() example:

title: Getting Started with nysOpenData
text: functions provide access NY State Open Data, serve slightly different purposes. general: Use nys_pull_dataset() dataset available nys_list_datasets() Use nys_any_dataset() working datasets outside catalog Together, functions allow users either quickly access datasets flexibly query dataset available nys Open Data portal.

title: Getting Started with nysOpenData
text: Every day, New York State hosts Cash 4 Life lottery, dates winning numbers can found . R, nysOpenData package can used pull data directly. Let’s filter dataset include rows cash_ball “04”. nys_pull_dataset() function can filter based columns dataset. filter, add filters = list() put whatever filters like inside. colnames call , know column called “cash_ball” can use accomplish . Success! calling lottery_04 dataset see 2 rows data, distinct() call see cash_ball featured dataset “04.” can also add one criteria filtering. successfully filtered latest two rows winning number either “01” “02”
code: lottery_04 <- nys_pull_dataset(dataset = "kwxv-fwze",limit = 2, filters = list(cash_ball = "04")) lottery_04 #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   draw_date           winning_numbers cash_ball #>   <dttm>              <chr>               <dbl> #> 1 2026-02-21 00:00:00 20 25 30 52 55          4 #> 2 2026-02-17 00:00:00 11 22 32 56 60          4  # Checking to see the filtering worked lottery_04 |>   distinct(cash_ball) #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>   cash_ball #>       <dbl> #> 1         4 lottery_01_02 <- nys_pull_dataset(dataset = "kwxv-fwze",limit = 2, filters = list(cash_ball = c("01","02"))) lottery_01_02 #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   draw_date           winning_numbers cash_ball #>   <dttm>              <chr>               <dbl> #> 1 2026-02-20 00:00:00 02 28 49 50 56          1 #> 2 2026-02-18 00:00:00 03 28 38 40 49          1

title: Getting Started with nysOpenData
text: Now successfully pulled data R, let’s mini analysis using cash_ball column, figure top cash_ball winners. , create bar graph Cash Ball frequencies. Frequency Cash Ball numbers among recent New York Cash 4 Life lottery winning tickets. Bars ordered least frequent highlight distribution outcomes. graph shows us cash balls won, many won.
code: # Visualizing the distribution, ordered by frequency  lottery <- nys_pull_dataset(dataset = "kwxv-fwze",limit = 50)  lottery |>   count(cash_ball) |>   ggplot(aes(     x = n,     y = reorder(cash_ball, n)   )) +   geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +   theme_minimal() +   labs(     title = "Top 50 Cash Ball Numbers in Winning Lottery Tickets",     x = "Number of Winners",     y = "Cash Ball"   )

title: Getting Started with nysOpenData
text: nysOpenData package serves robust interface NY State Open Data portal, streamlining path raw city APIs actionable insights. abstracting complexities data acquisition—pagination, type-casting, complex filtering—allows users focus analysis rather data engineering. demonstrated vignette, package provides seamless workflow targeted data retrieval, automated filtering, rapid visualization.

title: nysOpenData
text: nysOpenData provides simple, reproducible access datasets NY State Open Data platform — directly R, API keys manual downloads required. package available CRAN. Version 0.1.2 introduces streamlined, catalog-driven interface NYS Open Data. package provides three core functions: nys_list_datasets() — Browse available datasets live nys Open Data catalog nys_pull_dataset() — Pull cataloged dataset key, filtering, ordering, optional date controls nys_any_dataset() — Pull NYS Open Data dataset directly via Socrata JSON endpoint Datasets pulled via nys_pull_dataset() automatically apply sensible defaults catalog (default ordering date fields), still allowing user control : limit filters date / / order clean_names coerce_types redesign reduces maintenance burden, improves extensibility, provides scalable interface working nys Open Data. functions return clean tibble outputs support filtering viafilters = list(field = "value").

title: nysOpenData
text: nysOpenData makes New York State’s civic datasets accessible students, educators, analysts, researchers unified user-friendly R interface. Developed support reproducible research, open-data literacy, real-world analysis.

title: nysOpenData
text: RSocrata package provides general interface Socrata-backed portal, nysOpenData specifically tailored New York State Open Data. package part broader ecosystem tools working New York open data: nycOpenData — streamlined access NYC Open Data nysOpenData — streamlined access NY State Open Data mtaOpenData — streamlined access MTA-related NY State Open Data chiOpenData — streamlined access Chicago-related City Open Data laOpenData — streamlined access Los Angeles-related City Open Data Together, packages provide consistent, user-friendly interface working civic data across jurisdictions. Ease Use: need hunt 4x4 dataset IDs (e.g., kwxv-fwze); use catalog-based keys instead. Open Literacy: Designed specifically students researchers lower barrier entry civic data analysis.

what: Pull any New York State Open Data dataset from a Socrata JSON endpoint — nys_any_dataset
title: Pull any New York State Open Data dataset from a Socrata JSON endpoint — nys_any_dataset
text: Downloads data New York State Open Data Socrata JSON endpoint returns result tibble. function useful datasets included curated catalog returned [nys_list_datasets()].

title: Pull any New York State Open Data dataset from a Socrata JSON endpoint — nys_any_dataset
code: nys_any_dataset(   json_link,   limit = 10000,   timeout_sec = 30,   clean_names = TRUE,   coerce_types = TRUE )

title: Pull any New York State Open Data dataset from a Socrata JSON endpoint — nys_any_dataset
text: json_link single Socrata dataset JSON endpoint URL, `"https://data.ny.gov/resource/28gk-bu58.json"`. limit Number rows retrieve. Defaults 10,000. timeout_sec Request timeout seconds. Defaults 30. clean_names Logical. `TRUE`, column names converted snake_case using [janitor::clean_names()]. Defaults `TRUE`. coerce_types Logical. `TRUE`, package attempts lightweight, heuristic-based type coercion downloading data. Columns converted least 95 percent non-missing values can parsed target type. helps avoid unsafe conversions source data inconsistent.

title: Pull any New York State Open Data dataset from a Socrata JSON endpoint — nys_any_dataset
text: tibble containing rows requested New York State Open Data   endpoint.

title: Pull any New York State Open Data dataset from a Socrata JSON endpoint — nys_any_dataset
text: New York State Open Data datasets Socrata JSON endpoints usually follow pattern: `https://SOCRATA_DOMAIN/resource/<dataset_uid>.json` example, dataset Socrata UID `"28gk-bu58"` JSON endpoint: `https://data.ny.gov/resource/28gk-bu58.json` Users can find dataset's UID New York State Open Data Portal URL, API documentation page dataset, output [nys_list_datasets()]. `nys_any_dataset()` bypasses package catalog sends request directly supplied JSON endpoint. Unlike [nys_pull_dataset()], look defaults catalog keys, date fields, default ordering. function intended direct endpoint access. catalog-based workflows using readable keys Socrata UIDs, use [nys_pull_dataset()].

title: Pull any New York State Open Data dataset from a Socrata JSON endpoint — nys_any_dataset
code: # Examples that hit the live New York State Open Data API are guarded so CRAN # checks do not fail when the network is unavailable or slow. if (interactive() && curl::has_internet()) {   # Build a JSON endpoint from a Socrata UID   uid <- "abcd-1234"   endpoint <- paste0("https://data.ny.gov/resource/", uid, ".json")    out <- try(nys_any_dataset(endpoint, limit = 3), silent = TRUE)   if (!inherits(out, "try-error")) {     head(out)   } }

what: List available New York State Open Data datasets — nys_list_datasets
title: List available New York State Open Data datasets — nys_list_datasets
text: Retrieves live catalog New York State Open Data datasets New York State Open Data Portal catalog table (`ca8h-8gjq`) returns dataset metadata can used [nys_pull_dataset()].

title: List available New York State Open Data datasets — nys_list_datasets
List available New York State Open Data datasets — nys_list_datasets
text: tibble containing available New York State Open Data datasets. Important   columns include: key human-readable dataset key generated dataset name. uid official Socrata dataset identifier used New York State Open       Data. name dataset name New York State Open Data catalog. Additional metadata columns New York State Open Data catalog may also   returned.

title: List available New York State Open Data datasets — nys_list_datasets
text: returned tibble includes official Socrata dataset `uid` human-readable `key`. `uid` stable identifier used New York State Open Data Portal Socrata API, `key` generated dataset name using [janitor::make_clean_names()] make datasets easier reference R code, especially classroom reproducible research settings. users begin calling `nys_list_datasets()`, searching returned catalog dataset interest, passing either `key` `uid` [nys_pull_dataset()].

title: List available New York State Open Data datasets — nys_list_datasets
code: if (interactive() && curl::has_internet()) {   catalog <- nys_list_datasets()    # View available datasets   catalog    # Search for datasets containing a keyword   catalog[     grepl("KEYWORD", catalog$name, ignore.case = TRUE),     c("key", "uid", "name")   ] }

what: Pull a New York State Open Data dataset — nys_pull_dataset
title: Pull a New York State Open Data dataset — nys_pull_dataset
text: Downloads dataset New York State Open Data Socrata API using either human-readable catalog `key` official Socrata dataset `uid` returned [nys_list_datasets()].

title: Pull a New York State Open Data dataset — nys_pull_dataset
code: nys_pull_dataset(   dataset,   limit = 10000,   filters = list(),   date = NULL,   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   date_field = NULL,   where = NULL,   order = NULL,   timeout_sec = 30,   clean_names = TRUE,   coerce_types = TRUE )

title: Pull a New York State Open Data dataset — nys_pull_dataset
text: dataset single dataset `key` Socrata dataset `uid` [nys_list_datasets()]. example, key may look like `"example_dataset_name"`, UID may look like `"28gk-bu58"`. limit Number rows retrieve. Defaults 10,000. filters Optional named list exact-match filters. list name field name dataset, value value values match. Vector values translated SQL-style `` conditions generated SoQL query. example, `filters = list(FILTER_FIELD = c("VALUE_1", "VALUE_2"))` returns rows `FILTER_FIELD` either `"VALUE_1"` `"VALUE_2"`. date Optional single date used match records day. Requires `date_field`. Optional start date, inclusive. Requires `date_field`. Optional end date, exclusive. Requires `date_field`. date_field Optional date datetime column use `date`, ``, ``. must supplied date filter used. Users can identify available date columns inspecting dataset New York State Open Data Portal pulling small sample `limit`. Optional raw SoQL `` clause advanced filtering. SoQL Socrata Query Language used New York State Open Data. `date`, ``, `` also supplied, generated conditions combined `` using ``. order Optional raw SoQL `ORDER ` clause, `"DATE_FIELD DESC"`. timeout_sec Request timeout seconds. Defaults 30. clean_names Logical. `TRUE`, column names converted snake_case using [janitor::clean_names()]. Defaults `TRUE`. coerce_types Logical. `TRUE`, package attempts lightweight, heuristic-based type coercion downloading data. Columns converted least 95 percent non-missing values can parsed target type. helps avoid unsafe conversions source data inconsistent.

title: Pull a New York State Open Data dataset — nys_pull_dataset
text: tibble containing rows requested New York State Open Data   dataset.

title: Pull a New York State Open Data dataset — nys_pull_dataset
text: catalog `key` supplied, `nys_pull_dataset()` first retrieves live New York State Open Data catalog look corresponding Socrata `uid`, sends second request download dataset . Supplying `uid` directly stable avoids ambiguity, keys provided readability classroom-friendly workflows. Dataset keys generated dataset names using [janitor::make_clean_names()]. keys derived live catalog metadata, Socrata UIDs stable identifiers. `nys_pull_dataset()` designed common catalog-based workflows. arbitrary Socrata JSON endpoints included package catalog, use [nys_any_dataset()]. `filters` argument intended simple exact-match filtering. complex conditions, use `` argument raw SoQL syntax. Internally, filter field names wrapped `TRIM()` constructing SoQL queries reduce mismatches caused leading trailing whitespace source data. Type coercion intentionally conservative. `coerce_types = TRUE`, package attempts infer common R column types API response, columns inconsistent values may remain character columns. Datetime coercion also conservative. Timezone offsets sub-second precision may always preserved automatic parsing, columns inconsistent datetime formats may remain character columns.

title: Pull a New York State Open Data dataset — nys_pull_dataset
code: if (interactive() && curl::has_internet()) {   # Pull by human-readable key   nys_pull_dataset("example_dataset_name", limit = 3)    # Pull by Socrata UID   nys_pull_dataset("28gk-bu58", limit = 3)    # Filter to one value   nys_pull_dataset(     "28gk-bu58",     limit = 3,     filters = list(award_name = "MBA")   )    # Filter to multiple values   nys_pull_dataset(     "28gk-bu58",     limit = 10,     filters = list(award_name = c("MBA", "BBA"))   )    # Date filtering   nys_pull_dataset(     "28gk-bu58",     from = "1976-01-01",     to = "1978-01-01",     date_field = "date_first_registered",     limit = 100   )    # Advanced filtering with raw SoQL   nys_pull_dataset(     "28gk-bu58",     where = "award_name = 'MBA' AND program_name = 'Accountancy'",     order = "date_first_registered DESC",     limit = 100   ) }
